close_notes: Etcd data base reached its space limit. Etcd team performed a defragmentation to free space on the data base. After this actions the issue was resolved.
customer_facing_impact:
- Users may experience issues with new endpoint Creation, Update to existing endpoints and Deletion of endpoints.
- Existing data paths are not impacted.
chronology:
Response Team Participants (add abbrev. role and name as needed)
=========================================================
SRE ServiceEndpoint (SE): Dilip D R, Gurdial Singh, Mohan Kumar P B, Kiranbabu R A
DEV ServiceEndpoint: ARUN SEKAR
ETCD: Adrian Junea
ETCD MGR: Daniel ZHU, 
LBaaS: Matt Mulsow
SDE:Daniel ZHU
Leadership: 
Excecs: 
AVM(s):  James Gonzalez, Evelyn Artavia 
External:   
 
 
Slack Channel Details:  
#cloudserviceendpoint-cie -  https://ibm-cloudplatform.slack.com/archives/C020QNHJLF7/p1764874061857499
: 
 
Teams Mtg Details:  
https://teams.microsoft.com/meet/23750961467134?p=Nzr1cgbDD8FOiVJHB8
 
 
Times in UTC
[2025-Dec-4]
xxxx Impact Start
18:51 Sev3 pCIE Created
18:53 serviceendpoint team requesting support from etcd
19:03 SE trying auto-compaction
19:14 AVM James Joining teams call as AVM OnDuty
19:15 serviceendpoint team confirmed this issue is affecting new provisions. 
19:16 etcd team joined the call
19:18 LBaaS team joined
19:31 etcd recommending a rolling restart first. Also indicating 8.6GB is the limit for this solution. etcd is discontinued. SME for etcd is in Europe, there's no SME in americas atm so finding the right solution may take longer. ** possible Identification DIM.
19:35 etcd getting instructions on how to complete rolling restart.
19:38 etcd performing starting actions for rolling restart.
19:39 etcd confirmed connectivity to the db should not be lost while performing the rolling restart.
19:41 first replica completed the rolling restart successfully.
19:48 AVM Sev3 CIE Confirmed
19:53 The service team is currently reviewing the leader. they are also checking if we can we proceed with the de fragmentation, That might allow the pod or container that is failing to start due to this error to run successfully.
19:58 ETCD will implement the cleanup solution. When disk space runs out, ECD enters maintenance mode, requiring cleanup to restore normal operation
 20:06 the service team is now checking the dashboards.
20:10 The team checked the CRI and reviewed data size. Terminal access was confirmed, and cluster details showing members and disk size were examined. The new table was verified and works.
20:The team noted that in the C-PL1 process, Step 5 is the largest and most significant. SDE confirmed this occurs in the current workflow. The decision was made to proceed with the recommended approach.
20:17 The team agreed to exit the current session and start a quick huddle to work on database connectivity. They will return once the connection is established.
20:32 The ICD team attempted a rolling restart of the database, but it did not resolve the issue. They are still troubleshooting and joined another call to share credentials. Current observation: the ETCD database size is full, preventing new writes or updates. Existing reads are functioning, but write operations fail due to space exceeded errors, despite actual data size appearing normal.
21:11 The issue has been resolved, and the database is back online. The team will verify connectivity from their side.
21:16 The team confirmed the ETCD cluster has three members: two with large disk space and one with very small space. During the switchover, the smaller member was shut down, and leadership moved to M2. While focusing on defragmentation and password retrieval for M2, an automatic system-level switchover occurred, making M1 the current leader. Data is now synchronized across all three members, with relatively small sizes (tens of MB). The team requested verification that the latest data is intact and functioning correctly on all sides
21:19 The team is waiting for the CSE team to confirm if systems have returned to normal. Updates will be shared, and assistance will be provided if needed
21:25The team emphasized the importance of supporting this key IBM Cloud customer. Adrian will escalate the topic to the Postgres team, which has previously handled ETCD issues. The plan is to present the current situation, the solution implemented today, and the customer's concerns. Adrian will query the Postgres team for additional suggestions or actions that can be taken
21:26 Before leaving the meeting, the team identified the solution. They exited the call to proceed with internal password usage required for the next steps.
21:48 the issue has been resolved and mitigated. 
=============================
               FOR AVM REFERENCE
=============================
